---
title: "About"
lang: en
---

## Profile

::: {.section-intro}
I am a lecturer in mathematics and statistics in the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at Université Laval, in Quebec City. I also coordinate the Center for Tutoring and Learning in mathematics and statistics, a student success support service for several departmental courses.
:::

My work is situated at the intersection of university teaching, applied statistics, and digital tool development for teaching and research. I place central importance on reproducibility, open science, clear explanations, and accessible learning resources.

::: {.about-grid}
::: {.profile-panel}
### Teaching

Course design in statistics, data science, and R programming, with emphasis on active learning, realistic case studies, and reproducible reporting.
:::

::: {.profile-panel}
### Research

Development of methods and software for density estimation, multi-state models, circular data, hidden Markov models, and animal movement analysis.
:::

::: {.profile-panel}
### Digital tools

Creation of R packages, Quarto platforms, interactive tutorials, and open resources supporting reproducible research and data science teaching.
:::

::: {.profile-panel}
### Educational innovation

Careful integration of digital tools and conversational assistants into supervised learning settings, with human support remaining central.
:::
:::

## Recent highlights

- Development of [GPT-CDA](cda/index.qmd), featured in [ULaval News on May 22, 2026](https://nouvelles.ulaval.ca/2026/05/22/le-gpt-cda-un-robot-conversationnel-qui-epaule-les-etudiantes-et-etudiants-dans-les-cours-de-mathematiques-et-de-statistique-66c35c61-4649-41a0-befb-c2f79036ac30).
- Publication of [Beyond the next step: A multi-criteria generative validation framework for step selection functions](https://doi.org/10.1111/2041-210X.70313) in Methods in Ecology and Evolution.
- Release of recent R packages on CRAN, including [DonutMap](packages/donutmap.qmd), [ggcircular](packages/ggcircular.qmd), [CircularRegression](packages/circularregression.qmd), and [tutorizeR](packages/tutorizeR.qmd).
- Workshop on AI and teaching at SSC 2026, accompanied by a [public teaching-resource gallery](https://aureliennicosiaulaval.github.io/ssc-2026-ai-teaching-gallery/).
- 2026 New Investigator Presentation Award at SSC 2026 for a presentation on LOO-LBFP.

## Contact

::: {.meta-list}
Université Laval, Department of Mathematics and Statistics

Quebec City, QC, Canada

[aurelien.nicosia@mat.ulaval.ca](mailto:aurelien.nicosia@mat.ulaval.ca)
:::

## About this site

This website is built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org) and deployed with GitHub Pages. It gathers my research, teaching, software development, and educational innovation activities. The source repository is available from the navigation bar.

::: {.highlight-strip}
Corrections and suggestions are welcome through the GitHub interface of the site or by email.
:::
